gpt55_review_summary: Curie the 2nd found no correctness, compatibility, or metadata issues. The review confirmed that salts are deterministic per suite/dataset/example/arm/repeat, static mode remains the default, and Cachet handoff payload references remain unchanged while vLLM prefix-cache salts vary.
verification: poetry run pytest tests/test_benchmark_runner.py tests/test_openai_compatible.py tests/test_vllm_smoke.py -q -> 152 passed
poetry run pytest tests/test_public_package.py tests/test_project_governance.py -q -> 54 passed
poetry run python -m compileall -q src tests -> passed
poetry check -> passed with existing duplicated project/tool.poetry metadata warnings
changed-file Databricks credential pattern scan -> no matches
what_changed: Benchmark requests now carry a per-arm repeat_index so repeated measurements can be identified by request consumers.
OpenAI-compatible benchmarks support an opt-in prefix_cache_salt_mode=per_request that derives cache_salt per suite, dataset, example, arm, and repeat while preserving static mode by default.
Prepared vLLM smoke benchmarks now pass per_request salt mode so repeated Databricks runs avoid vLLM prefix-cache reuse while keeping the same Cachet handoff payload URI.
why: The successful Databricks g6.8xlarge/L4 run proved native vLLM loading but repeats 2 and 3 were dominated by vLLM prefix-cache hits for both baseline and Cachet arms, hiding payload URI cache behavior. Per-request salts make the next hot-payload run interpretable.
